Ad Text:
An exciting opportunity to join Woodside Energy, a global energy company with a 70‑year history and a strong focus on innovation, digital transformation, and the energy transition.

In this role, you will take a hands‑on, senior technical position designing, building, and evolving enterprise‑scale AI platform capabilities. You’ll focus on creating secure, scalable, production‑ready AI platform services that enable the delivery of Generative AI and advanced AI solutions across the business. You will own critical shared platform components and play a key role in shaping technical direction, standards, and best practices.

Key responsibilities include:

Designing and building reusable AI platform services, APIs, and frameworks
Developing scalable components for model hosting, inference, orchestration, and agentic systems
Integrating Generative AI capabilities (LLMs, prompt orchestration, vector databases) into production platforms
Ensuring platform resilience, security, observability, and Responsible AI compliance
Owning the technical health, evolution, and sustainability of AI platform services
Leading complex technical decisions and contributing to platform standards and governance
Providing technical leadership through mentoring, collaboration, and peer review
Key skills & experience:

10+ years’ experience in AI/ML with strong platform or backend engineering exposure
Strong hands‑on experience with Generative AI frameworks (Azure, AWS, OpenAI)
Proficiency in Python and API development (REST/GraphQL)
Experience with cloud platforms, containerisation (Docker), and CI/CD
Knowledge of model hosting, inference APIs, and vector databases
Strong engineering judgement, secure coding practices, and problem‑solving skills
Familiarity with Responsible AI and enterprise security standards
Additional Details

Start: Immediate Start
Location: Perth, CBD 
Flexibility: 2 days from home
If this role sounds like a strong match for your experience, please apply.

Note: As this is a contract position, applicants must be located in Perth, WA and hold full Australian work rights.

With over 90 years' combined experience, NES Fircroft (NES) is proud to be the world's leading engineering staffing provider spanning the Oil & Gas, Power & Renewables, Chemicals, Construction & Infrastructure, Life Sciences, Mining and Manufacturing sectors worldwide. With more than 80 offices in 45 countries, we are able to provide our clients with the engineering and technical expertise they need, wherever and whenever it is needed. We offer contractors far more than a traditional recruitment service, supporting with everything from securing visas and work permits, to providing market-leading benefits packages and accommodation, ensuring they are safely and compliantly able to support our clients.

ABOUT US:

Water Source Australia is a dynamic startup based in South Melbourne, dedicated to developing innovative water treatment solutions to solve the global drinking water crisis. Our team of experts is on the brink of bringing our first product to market, combining cutting-edge technology with a focus on efficiency and reliability. We collaborate closely with various stakeholders to ensure our solutions meet the highest standards and address the critical needs of today and tomorrow. Our commitment to excellence drives us to continuously innovate and refine our approaches.

We have developed the Brolga, a smart multi-barrier micro water treatment plant designed to produce safe, potable water from a wide range of non-potable sources. Capable of delivering up to 30,000 litres of treated water per day (average), the Brolga is ideally suited for supplying drinking water to small communities, remote facilities, and disaggregated or decentralised applications where conventional infrastructure is unavailable, unreliable, or economically unviable.

At the heart of the Brolga is an intelligent control system known as ELEIoT, which enables the unit to operate autonomously. The Brolga’s primary purpose is to eliminate pathogen contamination in source water, ensuring the consistent delivery of clean, safe water. Its proprietary operating logic, hosted on the integrated ELEIoT infrastructure, continuously monitors performance, anticipates in-field issues, and automatically resolves them without requiring human intervention.

Water Source Australia is proudly a part of the Adidem Group, who's business philosophy is focused on balancing three circles of responsibility: economic success, stakeholder fulfilment and positive social and environmental change.

JOB DESCRIPTION:

We are seeking a self-driven, technically proficient and experienced Verification and Validation (V&V) Engineer to join our dynamic team on a 12-month contract. Subject to business needs and the skills and performance of the successful candidate, there may be the opportunity for the contract to be converted into a permanent employment opportunity.

The successful candidate will be responsible for interpreting technical design specifications and descriptions, alongside the overarching V&V strategy, to develop and execute actionable testing plans and protocols.

This position requires a professional who is capable of high-level technical analysis and hands-on physical execution. You will ensure our products meet all requirements through a risk-based testing approach, supporting the product from the workshop to field trials and on-site servicing. Experience in highly regulated industries, knowledge of cloud computing, edge PCs, and PLCs is advantageous.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Review and interpret design output documentation, including mechanical and electrical drawings and software logic, to develop a thorough understanding of product specifications and potential failure modes.

Develop and execute verification and validation plans and protocols aligned with the V&V strategy, based on product requirements, design outputs, risk assessments and intended use cases.

Deliver formal and rigorous test reports detailing clear requirements traceability between design requirements, test cases, test results and non-conformances.

Lead technical investigations into product, subsystem and component failures, including troubleshooting, root cause analysis and corrective action recommendations.

Provide regular, customer-focused performance updates on field trial units to key stakeholders, translating complex field data, including Operational Intelligence and Business Intelligence metrics, into clear and accessible formats tailored to the audience.

Manage the practical deployment of units during field trials, including physical troubleshooting, routine on-site support and preventative maintenance, to ensure operational reliability.

Work closely with the engineering team to support the preparation of formal engineering and quality documentation, including risk assessments, technical reports, procedures and other controlled documents required for product development.

Support the development of user documentation, including manuals, service procedures and how-to guides.

Travel as required to manage unit installations, conduct performance monitoring and carry out essential on-site maintenance.

Interpret and ensure compliance with relevant industry standards and regulations.

QUALIFICATIONS, SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:

Minimum 5 years’ experience in verification, validation, testing, commissioning or systems integration, preferably within a regulated or quality-managed industry such as water, medical devices, pharmaceuticals, food and beverage, automotive or industrial automation.

Demonstrated experience integrating, verifying and validating complex, multi-disciplinary systems.

Understanding of risk-based testing methodologies.

Bachelor’s degree in Electronics Engineering, Mechatronics Engineering, Systems Engineering, Industrial Automation or a related discipline.

Demonstrated hands-on skills across industrial equipment, instrumentation, and control systems.

Experience with structured root cause analysis and corrective action processes.

Strong capability in interpreting technical documentation across mechanical, electrical and software domains.

Proficiency in interpreting and applying industry standards and regulations.

Experience working within a Quality Management System and producing controlled technical documentation, including requirement traceability.

Ability to work independently, manage priorities and deliver outcomes with limited supervision.

Strong communication and teamwork skills, with the ability to build positive rapport with customers and external stakeholders.

Must have appropriate working rights in Australia.

Must have an Australian driver’s licence.

DESIRABLE:

Experience with PLCs, industrial control systems and instrumentation.

Experience with cloud-connected or edge-based systems.

Familiarity with Python, C++ or similar languages.

Experience with test data analysis, logging systems and technical reporting tools.

Experience supporting field trials, commissioning or service activities for deployed equipment.




To apply please respond to this job ad. 

Applications close 1st of May 2026.

Automation Engineer – Renewables

Cromarty Automation | Port Melbourne, Australia




About Us

Cromarty Automation is a trusted leader in industrial process control solutions, delivering cutting-edge automation and electrical engineering services to Australian and international clients. With offices in Brisbane, Melbourne, Hobart, Launceston, and Devonport, we’ve built a reputation for innovation and reliability over 30+ years.




Our expertise spans automation engineering, instrumentation, and technical services across diverse industries, including renewable energy. Our mission is to be Australia’s leading independent automation provider—driving operational excellence for our clients and creating positive impact through the strength of our people.




The Opportunity

We’re seeking an experienced Automation Engineer (Renewables) to join our team in Melbourne. This is your chance to work on high-impact renewable energy projects, leveraging your technical skills in a collaborative, forward-thinking environment.

You’ll be part of a highly skilled engineering team, contributing to projects that shape a sustainable future while advancing your own professional growth.




What You’ll Do

As an Automation Engineer at Cromarty, you will:

Deliver automation solutions for renewable energy and other industries.

Provide technical design expertise aligned with Cromarty standards, QA processes, and Australian regulations.

Manage the full project lifecycle—from concept and tender through to design, programming, configuration, and commissioning.

Apply automation best practices, containerisation, and cloud-hosted deployments to ensure robust, scalable solutions.




What We’re Looking For

To thrive in this role, you’ll bring:

Bachelor of Engineering in Electrical, Mechatronics, Automation, or IoT/IT.

Industry experience in Renewable Energy (minimum 3 years).

Strong background in Automation, CI/CD processes, and Ignition.

Hands-on experience with containerisation and orchestration (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes).

Proven ability in cloud solution deployment (AWS, Azure, or similar).

Proficiency in programming languages such as C#, Java, or Python.

Knowledge of network architecture and cybersecurity principles.

Australian working rights (mandatory).




Why Join Cromarty?

Competitive salary package.

Work on rewarding, sustainability-focused projects with advanced technologies.

Hybrid work flexibility in a supportive, innovative team culture.

Clear opportunities for professional growth and career progression.

About us. 

Urbis is more than just a consultancy.   We are a multidisciplinary urban consultancy, inspiring and creating solutions for our ever-changing world.   We empower our brightest minds with opportunities to push boundaries, collaborate and deliver exceptional, creative solutions for our clients and each other.

If you're looking to make transformational impact in our cities and communities, we're keen to meet you. 

We pride ourselves on empowering our employees and supporting them to be the best they can be. Here you will make a tangible difference and create positive impact, while gaining enviable industry experience and broad exposure to the most exciting work.

The opportunity & how you will make an impact. 

As our AI Solution Engineer, you will lead the advancement of our Generative AI platform, uKnow, built on Azure Cloud. This role will be responsible for designing, developing and implementing innovative AI products that enhance employee & client experience and drive efficiency within the organisation leveraging expertise in Azure AI services, machine learning and data engineering. Working within an established Data Engineering team of 5, you will participate in a large variety of exciting and challenging projects. You'll make an impact by:

Designing, building and deploying Generative AI solutions on Azure, including AI-powered copilots/agents built with Microsoft Copilot Studio, that enhance employee and client experience
Translating business needs into scalable AI and data solutions by collaborating with cross-functional teams across Urbis & Cistri
Building and optimising data pipelines and models, ensuring high performance, reliability and efficiency in production environments
Driving continuous improvement by automating processes, enhancing data infrastructure and improving system scalability
Championing ethical AI practices, ensuring compliance with governance standards and contributing to responsible AI adoption
Contributing to the evolution of Urbis' AI platform (uKnow) and broader digital capacity
Coaching and mentoring individuals within the team

About you. 

We look for talented people who are purpose-driven and thrive in high-performing, inclusive and collaborative environments.  You'll work across teams to bring out the best in others whilst bringing new thinking and energy.

We know everyone is different and bring different strengths and passions with them.  In addition to yours, we're keen to connect with you if you bring the following experiences and attributes:

A tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Statistics, Informatics, Information Systems or another quantitative field coupled with a minimum of 5 years' experience in a Data Engineering role
Proven experience designing and implementing AI/ML solutions using Azure AI services and Large Language Models.
Experience using AI-powered copilots/agents using Microsoft Copilot Studio is highly regarded
A proven high proficiency manipulating data in the MS Azure cloud platform and a successful history of linking, manipulating, processing and extracting value from large, disconnected datasets in prior roles.
Strong data engineering capability, including working with large, complex datasets using Python, SQL and modern data platforms
A proactive mindset, with a strong interest in emerging AI technologies and industry best practices
A collaborative mindset, to bring out the best in yourself and others while bringing new thinking to Urbis and our clients
Passion for purpose-led work, and driving a positive legacy for our communities, clients and yourself
Authenticity to foster respect, trust and advocacy in all your work interactions
Confidence and decisiveness to drive high performance for yourself, others and our clients
Innovation and creativity when solving complex problems
A digitally curious mindset, that is keen to explore how technology can contribute to our experience and efficiency

Why Urbis? 

We are passionate about purpose with impact. You'll join an organisation united by a shared goal: shaping cities and communities for a better future. Your work will help create a positive legacy through innovative and meaningful contributions.  You're encouraged to bring your ideas, be bold and contribute to an environment built on connection, flexibility and authenticity.

We invest in your growth, with inspirational leadership experiences, structured development pathways and the freedom to lead your own way. We empower you to shape your career and your impact.
We believe in flexible benefits, performance-driven rewards and experiences that support you both professionally and personally—because what matters to you, matters to us.  

Sound interesting?

If we sound like a great fit for you, and you are ready to make an impact, we'd love to hear from you. 

Click on ‘Apply' for this job.' You can also confidentially connect with us via careers@urbis.com.au

#cityshapers #communitymakers #lifeaturbis

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

The team

The AI Platform Services (AIPS) teams build platforms and solutions that solve complex information problems and enable AI innovation across the SEEK Group. Our AI Platform Engineering teams provide shared infrastructure, governance, and reusable assets that empower AI and product teams to experiment safely, ship faster, and deliver intelligent experiences at scale.

Teams are cross-functional, multi-disciplinary squads operating within an APAC matrix organisational structure. We work in an Agile environment with regular standups, sprint ceremonies, and collaborative ways of working.

About the role 

As a Senior Staff Engineer at SEEK, you'll be part of a fast-paced and supportive environment where you can make a real difference. You'll work on complex challenges that have a direct impact on people's lives, helping us evolve our business and reach new heights.

This role is a senior technical specialist within our AI Platform Services (AIPS) Engineering teams, focused on AI data infrastructure. Reporting to the Head of Engineering, you will be the authority on centralised data platforms for AI/ML workloads, proactively understanding the problems across our AI teams, developing the strategy, advocating for solutions, and delivering outcomes that accelerate AI innovation across SEEK.

Responsibilities 

Define and own the technical strategy for centralised AI data infrastructure across AIPS
Design scalable data architectures for AI/ML workloads spanning ingestion, transformation, and serving
Consult with our AI teams to deeply understand data needs and translate them into strategic platform solutions
Drive data governance, quality, and consistency across AI/ML data pipelines
Guide Staff Engineers and engineering teams on AI engineering best practices and patterns
Partner closely with Architecture, Data Services, and Security on data platform decisions
Advocate for and embed data engineering practices that reduce duplication and increase self-service capability
Support the Head of Engineering in capacity planning, scoping, and prioritisation of AI platform work, while contributing to the broader AIPS engineering community through knowledge sharing and technical leadership
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Experience delivering data solutions to real-time ML systems, with a strong understanding of how data engineering overlaps with ML engineering
Deep data engineering experience across pipelines, data modelling, feature engineering, and data governance at scale
Strong infrastructure experience with data-focused services and Infrastructure-as-Code practices
Python proficiency for data platform tooling, pipelines, and integrations
Demonstrated experience defining and implementing technical strategy that drives cultural and organisational change in the data space, underpinned by a strong understanding of MLOps and the ML lifecycle
Feature store and vector store implementation experience (or a demonstrated desire to build expertise in this space), along with MLOps or ML lifecycle experience spanning experiment tracking, model deployment, and training-serving consistency
Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and cloud-agnostic data technologies, building self-service data capabilities for technical teams, and SRE or operational excellence experience for data platform systems
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

About the role 

As a Lead Engineer at SEEK, you'll be part of a fast-paced and supportive environment where you can make a real difference. You'll work on complex challenges that have a direct impact on people's lives, helping us evolve our business and reach new heights.

This role provides technical and people leadership within our AI Platform Engineering domain — managing, mentoring, and developing the engineers who build the platforms powering AI innovation across SEEK. Reporting to the Engineering Manager, you will partner closely with the Staff Platform Engineer on technical direction and collaborate with Data Scientists, ML Engineers, and product teams to deliver scalable, resilient AI platform solutions. You will have 3–6 direct reports.

Responsibilities 

Lead and develop a high-performing team through coaching, mentoring, career development, and a culture of safety, inclusivity, and engagement
Drive delivery of AI platform systems from development through to production, ensuring high availability, resilience, and performance for production AI workloads
Contribute to technical strategy for GenAI and ML platforms in close partnership with the Staff Platform Engineer
Build and operate scalable AI infrastructure on AWS, leveraging Python and Infrastructure-as-Code
Strengthen engineering practices and DevOps culture for AI platform operations, including incident management, on-call processes, and supporting documentation
Build strong relationships with key stakeholders including Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Architecture, and Security
Lead recruitment, onboarding, and retention of engineering talent
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

Direct line management experience of engineers, including coaching, mentoring, and career development
Strong AWS infrastructure experience across compute, storage, networking, and security, with Infrastructure-as-Code practices
Python proficiency for platform automation, tooling, and integrations
Experience building and operating highly available, resilient production systems
Demonstrated experience writing well-tested, readable software using secure coding practices, current patterns, and tooling
Hands-on ML Engineering or MLOps experience, including GenAI/LLM platforms such as model inference, vector databases, RAG patterns, and AI Gateways, with multi-cloud experience beyond AWS (e.g. GCP/Vertex AI)
SRE experience building and operating production platform systems, with a track record of enabling technical teams (Data Scientists, ML Engineers) through platform capabilities and experience recruiting and developing software engineers
Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.

Job Title: Data Engineer (AWS, Lambda, S3 and Terraform)
Company: Sirius Technology Sydney part of Sirius People Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
We are seeking an ambitious and self-starting Data Engineer to join our client’s team during a high-growth phase. As they kick off Phase 2 delivery, you will play a critical role in maintaining, optimizing, and evolving their AWS platform.
Our client values innovation and efficiency. They are looking for someone who doesn't just "do the job" but actively leverages AI tools to automate workflows and drive results faster.

Please note Melbourne-based candidates with full time works rights only will be considered for this role as it is a requirement for this position and no sponsorship is on offer. 

The Technical Skillset 

To be successful in this role, you will need deep hands-on experience with:

Data Integration: Expert-level development using the Matillion platform to orchestrate complex ETL/ELT workflows.
AWS Data Ecosystem: Proven expertise in S3 (Data Lake architecture), AWS Glue (Cataloging and Jobs), and Lambda (Serverless functions).
Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Robust experience writing and maintaining Terraform and CloudFormation scripts to ensure "Infrastructure as Code" standards.
Scripting & SQL: Advanced SQL for data transformation and Python for Lambda development and automation tasks.
Data Modeling: Solid understanding of Data Warehousing concepts (Star/Snowflake schema) and data partitioning strategies.
Key Responsibilities & Tasks
Pipeline Maintenance: Take ownership of existing Matillion and AWS pipelines, ensuring high availability and resolving bottlenecks.
Phase 2 Delivery: Design and deploy new data features and integrations to support the next stage of the business roadmap.
Automation: Utilize Terraform/CloudFormation to automate the deployment of cloud resources, reducing manual intervention.
Performance Tuning: Optimize SQL queries and AWS Glue jobs to improve processing speed and reduce cloud consumption costs.
Security & Governance: Implement and manage IAM roles, encryption, and data lifecycle policies within the AWS environment.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with the wider team to cross-skill, share knowledge, and ensure architectural alignment.
What You’ll Bring
Innovator’s Mindset: You are enthusiastic about achieving new things and aren't afraid to suggest better ways of working.
AI-Driven Efficiency: You are a modern engineer who leverages AI (LLMs, Copilots) to be as productive and efficient as possible.
Self-Starter Attitude: You can take a high-level brief and run with it, ensuring the platform remains stable and scalable.
Industry Agnostic: Background in any industry is welcome—it's your technical craft and mindset that matter.
The Opportunity

While the initial contract is for 3–6 months, our client has a history of retaining high-performing contractors for up to a year. This is a chance to work in a collaborative, office-based environment where your contributions directly impact the success of a major project phase.

If you are a passionate Data Engineer with experience on AWS S3, Lambda and ideally Matillion, we encourage you to apply today via the link provided! 

Candidates from all backgrounds are welcome to apply too. Please feel free to also send a CV to kgonzalez@siriustechnology.com.au, our Principal IT Consultant for Data, AI and Cyber, thanks.

The Company
Join a small, senior team of software engineers in a well funded company, reporting directly to the engineering co-founders. Own features and help steer the direct of our ML product used by companies in Australia and the UK. And work on a product that users love. 

About You

The Opportunity

Contract: 6 months, with potential for extension

Location: Melbourne, Hybrid

We’re seeking a Systems Monitoring Engineer (Zabbix) to operate and enhance our client’s Zabbix‑based monitoring platform. This role focuses on improving visibility, reliability, and alert quality across Linux systems and network infrastructure.

This role is well suited to someone early in their career who has a strong technical foundation, enjoys working with Linux and networks, and is keen to learn how monitoring supports reliable IT systems.

You will work with more experienced engineers to configure monitoring checks, dashboards, and alerts, investigate issues raised by monitoring, and gradually build a strong understanding of our client’s systems and environment.

What You’ll Do

Operate, maintain, and enhance the Zabbix monitoring platform.
Configure and tune monitoring checks, triggers, dashboards, and alerts for Linux servers and network devices.
Investigate alerts, incidents, and performance trends to identify root causes and support resolution.
Work with infrastructure, network, and systems teams to address issues and improve monitoring coverage.
Improve alert quality by reducing noise and ensuring alerts are meaningful and actionable.
Expand monitoring for new and existing systems and services as environments evolve.
Document monitoring standards, procedures, and improvements to support shared understanding.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in system reliability and observability.

What You’ll Bring

Hands‑on experience with IT monitoring tools, ideally Zabbix.
A strong understanding of monitoring metrics for:
Linux/Unix systems (CPU, memory, disk, processes, services)
Network devices (latency, throughput, packet loss, interface health)
Solid Linux administration skills.
Working knowledge of IP networking concepts.
Ability to investigate and troubleshoot technical issues across systems and networks.
An organised and self‑directed approach to learning and working within complex environments.
The ability to collaborate effectively within teams with diverse skill sets and experience levels.
